description_long: This object is a compact digital multimeter, commonly used for electrical measurements in urban environments such as garages, workshops, or maintenance rooms. The main body is rectangular and made of black plastic, featuring a printed interface with various measurement settings and symbols. Two insulated test probes, one red and one black, are attached to the device via flexible cables and are neatly wrapped around the body for storage. The probes have pointed metal tips for making contact with electrical circuits, and the cables are secured with molded connectors. The device also features a rotary dial and several physical buttons for selecting measurement modes, as well as a small display screen for reading values. The overall design is utilitarian, with clear labeling and robust construction for frequent handling.
description: A compact digital multimeter with a black plastic body, red and black test probes, and a printed interface for electrical measurements. The probes are wrapped around the device, which features a rotary dial, buttons, and a small display screen.
description_view_0: Side view showing the flat rectangular body, the coiled red and black test leads, and the pointed metal tips of the probes.
description_view_1: End view highlighting the bundled cables and the connectors attached to the device, with the probe tips less visible.
description_view_2: Angled view displaying the rotary dial, printed interface, and the arrangement of the test leads across the device.
description_view_3: Front view focusing on the pointed tips of the red and black probes extending outward, with the main body and cable arrangement visible behind.
